> Clever Complete Agreement Required
> 
> [](#clever-complete-agreement-required)
> 
> This feature is included with a Clever Complete subscription. Sign up [here](https://www.clever.com/developer-signup) or email your Application Success Manager to learn more.

> 🚧
> 
> ### 
> 
> v3.1 Only
> 
> [](#v31-only)
> 
> LMS Connect API endpoints are only available on v3.1 of the Clever API. If you are interested in getting started with LMS Connect, reach out to your Application Success Manager (for existing partners) or to [\[email protected\]](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#4c2d3c3c20252f2d3825232261252238293e293f380c2f20293a293e622f2321).

> ❗️
> 
> ### 
> 
> This product is only available to existing Secure Sync partners.
> 
> [](#this-product-is-only-available-to-existing-secure-sync-partners)
> 
> To learn more about Secure Sync, please review [this documentation](https://dev.clever.com/docs/district-getting-started).

# 

Table of Contents

[Prerequisites](https://dev.clever.com/docs/lms-connect-beginners-guide#prerequisites)  
[Getting Started](https://dev.clever.com/docs/lms-connect-beginners-guide#getting-started)

# 

Prerequisites

[](#prerequisites)

You will not be able to complete this guide without having met the requirements below. Please take the time to read through each resource before you proceed.

-   Access to Clever's [Postman Collection](https://www.postman.com/clever-pe/developer-collections/overview?sideView=agentMode).
-   Follow this guide: [Exploring the Clever API](https://dev.clever.com/docs/exploring-the-clever-api).
-   Baseline understanding of [LMS Connect](https://dev.clever.com/docs/lms-connect-overview).

# 

Getting Started

[](#getting-started)

In this guide, we're going to be using the LMS Connect API to explore the gradebook sync integration for the LMSs listed below:

-   Google Classroom
-   Canvas
-   Schoology

Let's get familiarized with the LMS Connect Postman Collection. Go ahead and click through the folders and requests. Notice that there is a folder of identical requests for each LMS. They are labeled to help you differentiate between different LMS environments as you explore this product.

Click into **Environments > LMS Connect Demo**. You will find a set of LMS Connect-related variables.

_Please note that for the purposes of this guide, you should not need to edit any of these variables_.

![](https://files.readme.io/122a97d4a46aaa77ae19653fa7d03f59c49ccd447e180b0017a32c2cb4f37ea1-image.png)

* * *

# 

Testing It Out

[](#testing-it-out)

Expand each step below to view the required API calls and sample payloads.

Creating an Assignment  

We're going to get started by creating an assignment in _Google Classroom_. If you are more interested in trying this out with _Schoology_ or _Canvas_, the steps for creating and managing **assignments** and **submissions** will be the same. There will be a component of this activity where you will be checking your work in the actual LMS. You will find the necessary details for each LMS in the relevant sections at the bottom of the page.

Click into the POST _GC/Create Assignment_ Request of the **Google Classroom** folder. Make sure that you have the Environment set to "Development" in the top right corner. The authorization should already be set in the parent folder. This bearer token is provided by Clever for demo purposes only.

Notice that there is an example request body already included in the "Body" tab of the request.

![Description of the image](https://files.readme.io/d5f38950a7582c56844633c045355308673bdd90c953919282cc3ae6e7cb4e67-image.png)

Use the [data model](#) to adjust this request body however you'd like. Try editing the "title" field to something memorable. You will use this value to confirm the creation of your assignment later. When you are ready, click "Send".

You should receive a status `200` and a response body as seen below. **Be sure to save the "id" value somewhere for safekeeping.** You will need it later for other tasks. If you lose it, there is currently no way to view all assignments so you will just have to generate a new assignment to move on.

![Description of the image](https://files.readme.io/8541e396057e2acf39f8c28b01046845b2fa157625978a8bf4f22d486a5e0b80-image.png)

Be sure to refer to [this section](#) to check your work.

We've now created an assignment for a specific section in Clever and passed it along to Google Classroom. All students enrolled in that section will have a submission record associated with this assignment. We will explore submissions in a later step.

  
Editing an Assignment  

Now that we've created an assignment, what if there are things we want to change? For example, let's say we want to update the assignment "title", the "due date", and the "points\_possible" value?

Click into _PATCH GC/Update Assignment_. The request body should already be pre-populated with an update to the "due\_date" value. Let's add some more updates.

```json
{
  "title": "Second Title",
  "due_date": "2032-02-29T23:48:24.000Z",
  "points_possible": 50
}
```

In the request URL, take your **assignment ID** from the last step and assign it to the `temp_assignment_id` variable by hovering over it and pasting the value.

![Description of the image](https://files.readme.io/9338f0f6e09fa3a3c8052e2b7cdc3fe5efe6f3a7a996bf626b8fc3c84da15679-image.png)

When you are ready, click "Send". You should receive a status `200` as well as a response body including the updated record. Back in Google Classroom, you should see the updates as well.

  
Viewing Submissions
